item.text: {"summary":"Live-search review suggests one still-distinct initial direction: fold-safe parametric class-density features inspired by SDSS quasar probability selection methods that model class likelihoods in photometric flux/color space.","hypotheses":[{"title":"Class-Conditional Photometric Density Ratios","summary":"Fit fold-safe class-wise Gaussian density models in color-redshift space and use their log-likelihood ratios, posterior margins, and entropy as compact supervised statistical features.","feature_family":"fold_safe_parametric_class_density_features","feature_strategy":"Build a standardized compact space from ugriz magnitudes, adjacent and broad color indices, redshift, alpha/delta, and one-hot spectral_type/galaxy_population. Within each CV fold, fit small class-conditional GaussianMixture or regularized Gaussian density models separately for GALAXY, STAR, and QSO using only fold-training rows. Transform validation rows into per-class log likelihoods, log-likelihood ratios such as QSO-vs-STAR and GALAXY-vs-QSO, posterior-like normalized probabilities with empirical class priors, max posterior, entropy, and top-two margin. For test rows, refit the density models on all training data and generate the same features. Keep this as an added feature block rather than replacing raw photometry.","baseline_model_panel":"Use the same simple balanced panel across initial feature searches: a balanced linear classifier, a shallow/randomized tree ensemble, and one class-weighted gradient-boosted tree family such as LightGBM or CatBoost with balanced multiclass weighting.","model_panel_rationale":"The density-ratio block should be visible to linear models if it creates near-Bayes decision coordinates, while tree and boosting models can test whether nonlinear interactions between density scores and the original photometric/categorical fields add value.","validation_strategy":"5-fold StratifiedKFold scored by balanced accuracy. All class-density features for training rows must be generated out-of-fold; only the final test transform may use density models fit on the full training set.","materialization_hint":"Implement this as a preprocessing stage that first builds a small numeric design matrix, imputes/scales from fold-training statistics, fits one density estimator per class with conservative component counts and covariance regularization, then appends OOF/test density-score columns to the ordinary raw/categorical feature table before training the baseline panel.","expected_signal":"If useful, CV should show better QSO and STAR recall and improved balanced accuracy, especially for linear and shallow tree models; logs should show stable class likelihood margins rather than extreme singular-covariance scores.","novelty_confidence":"medium","risk":"This overlaps conceptually with prior local class posterior features, but differs by using global parametric class likelihoods rather than neighbor posteriors or graph propagation. Main risks are covariance instability, overfitting rare QSO regions, and weak gain if gradient boosting already reconstructs these density boundaries from raw colors.","sources":["https://arxiv.org/abs/1011.6392","https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.11724","https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.10963"]}]}
